visual_system_principles.goal: Keep the same project identity while making each exercise immediately legible, biomechanically plausible, and visually consistent across start, middle, and end frames.

four_prompt_questions: What must stay visible for someone to understand the exercise immediately?
Which body parts determine whether the execution looks correct?
Is there external support, and if yes, where is it, how is it touched, and what is it doing?
What visibly changes between the initial, middle, and final phase?
approval_checklist: movement readable in one or two seconds

camera_presets.frontal: front view, eye-level, shoulders square to the camera, movement centered and symmetrical
camera_presets.frontal_3q: slight 3/4 front view, eye-level, both sides of the body still readable
camera_presets.lateral_3q: slight 3/4 lateral view, eye-level, depth and alignment visible without losing the support object
camera_presets.lateral: clean side profile, eye-level, head-to-feet alignment clearly readable
camera_presets.floor_3q_high: 3/4 view from slightly above the floor, full mat visible, all limbs readable
camera_presets.floor_lateral: side view for floor exercise, camera slightly above mat height, full body and full mat visible

camera_detail_presets.standing_front_cardio: camera at lower chest height, stable full-body distance, natural lens without wide-angle distortion, level horizon, extra side space for steps and arm swing
camera_detail_presets.standing_front_mobility: camera at chest height, full-body distance with calm headroom, natural lens, level horizon, torso rotation or gentle mobility remains readable
camera_detail_presets.standing_front_reset: camera at chest height, centered and calm full-body framing, natural lens, level horizon, enough negative space for hands near ribs and breathing focus
camera_detail_presets.standing_lateral_strength: camera at hip-to-waist height, side or 3/4 side distance that keeps feet and hips fully visible, natural lens, level horizon, preserve alignment readability
camera_detail_presets.standing_lateral_support: camera at hip-to-waist height, 3/4 side distance that keeps both body and support fully visible, natural lens, level horizon, no perspective drift between frames
camera_detail_presets.standing_lateral_mobility: camera at waist height, side or 3/4 side distance that keeps pelvis and trunk readable, natural lens, level horizon, avoid dramatic depth distortion
camera_detail_presets.standing_lateral_balance: camera at ankle-to-waist friendly side distance, natural lens, level horizon, feet and support edge remain readable without crop
camera_detail_presets.standing_lateral_push: camera at mid-torso height, side or 3/4 side distance that shows hand contact, body line, and support surface together, natural lens, level horizon
camera_detail_presets.floor_high_control: camera slightly above floor level, 3/4 angle, full mat and full limb reach visible, natural lens, level horizon, preserve left/right limb relationships
camera_detail_presets.floor_side_alignment: camera slightly above mat height, clean side view, full body and mat visible, natural lens, level horizon, preserve line from shoulders through hips

framing_presets.full_body: full body visible with small headroom and both feet fully visible
framing_presets.full_body_headroom: full body visible with extra space above the head and around the limbs
framing_presets.full_body_side_feet_visible: full body in side or 3/4 side view with both feet, knees, and hips fully visible
framing_presets.full_body_support_visible: full body visible with the support object fully visible and uncropped
framing_presets.full_body_floor_mat_visible: full body on the mat with the full mat edges, hands, feet, and limb reach visible

support_object_presets.none: no external support object; the body and environment alone must explain the movement
support_object_presets.mat_only: exercise mat is part of the scene and must stay fully readable when the body is on the floor
support_object_presets.optional_balance_support: optional stable support may appear only if needed for balance; if present it must look intentional, properly scaled, and consistently placed
support_object_presets.chair_front_balance_both_hands: standard dining chair centered in front of the character, chair back around lower chest height, both palms resting lightly on the top edge, support used for balance and depth reference only
support_object_presets.chair_front_balance_one_hand: standard dining chair or stable support placed beside the front hand in a short split stance, one hand touches lightly for balance, support must stay aligned and properly scaled
support_object_presets.elevated_surface_two_hands: stable elevated surface such as bench, table, or sturdy counter, both palms planted symmetrically, support height remains fixed across the sequence and defines exercise load
support_object_presets.wall_two_hands: vertical wall plane with both palms clearly contacting it at chest to shoulder height, wall angle and hand spacing stay constant across the sequence

frame_strategies.1_static: one unique image is enough; middle and final can reuse the closest valid frame if budget is limited
frame_strategies.2_transition: initial and final should be unique; middle is optional and can reuse the closest valid frame
frame_strategies.3_motion: all three frames should be unique because the movement loses clarity without progression

exercises.support_object_visibility: chair back and both hands on the chair must stay fully visible
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Whole foot stays grounded, hips initiate backward, knees track with toes, torso inclines slightly, and the chair stabilizes balance without pulling the body upward.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: hips move back before depth increases, knees bend softly, and chair contact stays light and constant
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: depth increases without losing heel contact, chest stays open, and the chair remains centered as balance support

clarity_checks: the chair contact and support role are readable at a glance
heel grounding and squat depth are clearly visible
the image shows that the hips lead the descent, not the knees alone

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing upright, feet hip-width apart, knees extended but relaxed, hips stacked over heels, arms extended lightly holding the chair, neutral spine, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: hips moving back, knees softly bent, torso slightly inclined forward, arms still extended on the chair, heels grounded, neutral spine, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.fim: comfortable squat depth, hips back and lower than in the middle frame, knees bent without collapsing inward, chest open, heels grounded, hands still supporting on the chair, gaze forward

negative_constraints: do not crop the chair back
do not turn the movement into a deep athletic squat
do not hide heel contact
do not exaggerate lumbar arch

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Whole foot stays grounded, hips lead the motion, knees bend only through a conservative range, and the torso stays long and controlled.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: hips shift back first, knees soften, and the torso inclines slightly without changing the exercise into a full squat
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: depth increases only enough to show a partial squat, while alignment and heel contact remain clear

clarity_checks: the movement reads as partial rather than deep
the knees, hips, and feet remain fully readable
the image shows a controlled squat pattern without support

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing upright, feet hip-width apart, knees extended but relaxed, arms naturally in front for balance, neutral spine, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: hips slightly back, knees semiflexed, torso slightly inclined forward, arms balancing in front, heels grounded, neutral spine
phase_descriptions.fim: partial squat only, thighs clearly above parallel, hips back, knees aligned with feet, chest open, heels grounded, gaze forward

negative_constraints: do not show full-depth squat
do not let knees collapse inward
do not crop feet or knees
do not turn this into a jump squat

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object unless needed for balance
exercises.biomechanics_summary: A short split stance creates the base, the pelvis glides forward gently, the torso stays tall, and the stretch should read in the front hip rather than the lower back.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the pelvis shifts forward softly while the front knee bends slightly and the trunk stays upright
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the stretch becomes clearer through the front of the back-leg hip without deepening into an athletic lunge

clarity_checks: the split stance and front versus back leg roles are obvious
the trunk stays upright instead of collapsing forward
the final frame reads as a gentle stretch, not a strength lunge

phase_descriptions.inicio: split stance with short comfortable base, torso upright, both feet grounded, hips square, hands relaxed by the sides
phase_descriptions.meio: gentle forward shift of the pelvis, front knee softly bent, back leg long, torso upright, abdomen engaged, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.fim: clear but gentle hip stretch position, pelvis slightly forward, chest open, neutral spine, no pain expression, steady breathing posture

negative_constraints: do not force a deep lunge
do not overarch the lower back
do not hide pelvic alignment
do not turn this into an advanced flexibility pose

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: The hip hinge leads the fold, knees stay soft, the spine remains long, and the stretch should read in the back of the thighs rather than in a collapsed back.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the hips fold back, the torso inclines, and the hands travel down only as far as alignment stays readable
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the stretch deepens slightly through the posterior chain without locking the knees or collapsing the chest

clarity_checks: the exercise reads as a hamstring-focused hinge rather than a yoga forward fold
the knees stay slightly soft and visible
the spine line remains readable from hips to head

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing upright, feet hip-width apart, knees softly unlocked, arms relaxed, neutral spine, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: hips folding back into a gentle hinge, knees slightly bent, spine long, hands reaching toward thighs or shins without forcing
phase_descriptions.fim: mild hamstring stretch with hips back, torso inclined forward, knees still soft, neck relaxed, spine long rather than rounded hard

negative_constraints: do not lock the knees aggressively
do not collapse the chest onto the legs
do not round the back excessively
do not turn it into a full yoga fold

exercises.support_object_visibility: chair or stable support must stay fully visible near the front hand
exercises.biomechanics_summary: A short split stance controls the load, one light hand contact improves balance, both knees bend through a small range, and the torso stays upright over the base.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the body lowers only a little, the front and back knees soften, and the support hand remains fixed and readable
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the lunge depth becomes clear but stays short, with the pelvis centered and the support still functioning as balance aid

clarity_checks: the support position relative to the front hand is obvious
the movement reads as a short supported lunge, not a long athletic one
the front knee path and body balance are easy to read

phase_descriptions.inicio: short split stance, torso upright, both feet grounded, one hand lightly on the support, knees extended but relaxed, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: small lowering phase, front and back knees softly bending, pelvis centered, torso upright, support hand still visible
phase_descriptions.fim: short supported lunge depth only, front knee comfortably bent, back heel may be lifted naturally, torso tall, neutral spine, light support on the chair

negative_constraints: do not show a long athletic lunge
do not hide the support hand
do not let the front knee travel excessively forward
do not twist the torso away from the camera

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object; full mat edge should stay visible
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Hands and knees create a stable base, the core resists trunk rotation, opposite arm and leg reach long, and the pelvis stays level throughout the sequence.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: one limb pair starts reaching away while the supporting hand and knee stay rooted and the trunk stays steady
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the reach becomes longer and clearer without opening the hips or arching the lower back

clarity_checks: all support points and reaching limbs remain visible
the image shows opposite arm and leg action clearly
the pelvis and spine look stable instead of twisted

phase_descriptions.inicio: all fours on the mat, hands under shoulders, knees under hips, neutral spine, neck long, gaze down and slightly forward
phase_descriptions.meio: one arm sliding forward and opposite leg extending back, pelvis stable, trunk steady, support points still grounded
phase_descriptions.fim: full bird dog position, opposite arm and leg long, hips level, neutral spine, core engaged, gaze down slightly ahead

negative_constraints: do not rotate the hips open
do not overarch the lower back
do not crop hands, knees, or toes
do not turn the head sharply upward

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object; mat edges should remain visible
exercises.biomechanics_summary: The back stays supported, ribs stay controlled, opposite arm and leg move away from center, and the lower back must remain visibly stable against the floor.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: one arm and the opposite leg begin extending away while the bent tabletop shape remains readable on the other side
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the extension increases without losing lumbar control, rib position, or neck relaxation

clarity_checks: the lower-back control is visually believable
the opposite arm-leg pattern is obvious
hands, feet, and bent knee angles remain readable

phase_descriptions.inicio: lying on the back on the mat, hips and knees bent around tabletop, arms reaching up over the chest, head relaxed, ribs down
phase_descriptions.meio: one arm moving backward and opposite leg extending away, lower back staying close to the mat, abdomen engaged
phase_descriptions.fim: opposite arm and leg more extended than in the middle frame, core braced, lower back still controlled against the mat, neck relaxed

negative_constraints: do not arch the lower back off the mat
do not hide the bent knee angle
do not crop hands or feet
do not turn the pose into a dramatic ab crunch

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: The trunk stays upright, support alternates foot to foot, knees lift only to a low or medium height, and the landing stays soft with no running posture.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: one knee lifts while the opposite arm swings naturally and the support foot stays fully grounded
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the lifted side changes or reaches its clearest controlled height without creating a sprint or jump look

clarity_checks: the image reads as low-impact marching rather than running
the support foot and lifted knee remain fully visible
the trunk stays vertical and calm

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing upright, both feet on the floor, arms relaxed in marching position, chest open, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: one knee lifting gently to low or medium height, opposite arm naturally swinging, trunk upright, support foot grounded
phase_descriptions.fim: alternate side knee lift or the highest controlled knee raise of the movement, torso upright, no jumping, calm expression

negative_constraints: do not turn it into a running sprint pose
do not lift the knee excessively high
do not crop feet
do not show airborne jumping

exercises.support_object_visibility: support is optional; if used, hands and support edge must remain visible
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Weight transfers from whole foot to forefoot, ankles stay stacked, heels rise together, and the torso remains vertical without leaning into balance support.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the heels begin to rise while the body stays tall and the ankle line remains readable
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the heels lift higher but the body remains aligned, with no forward lean or collapsed ankles

clarity_checks: heel lift is clearly visible from side view
ankle stacking and balance look realistic
any optional support looks light, not load-bearing

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing upright with both feet flat, knees softly unlocked, arms relaxed or lightly supported, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: heels beginning to rise, weight moving to the balls of the feet, body stacked upright, ankles clearly visible
phase_descriptions.fim: heels lifted higher than in the middle frame, torso tall, legs straight but not locked, controlled balance, gaze forward

negative_constraints: do not crop heels or toes
do not turn the pose into tiptoe ballet styling
do not lean the torso forward
do not hide ankle position

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object; full mat should be visible
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Feet and shoulders create the base, the pelvis initiates a small lift, ribs stay controlled, and the range stays smaller than a full bridge.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the pelvis lifts off the mat gently while the shoulders stay anchored and the knees keep their line
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the top position becomes clearer without turning into a full high bridge or lumbar arch

clarity_checks: the movement reads as a short pelvic lift rather than a dramatic bridge
shoulder anchoring and foot placement remain visible
the top position stays controlled and realistic

phase_descriptions.inicio: lying on the back, knees bent, feet flat on the mat, arms by the sides, head relaxed, pelvis neutral
phase_descriptions.meio: pelvis lifting gently off the mat, knees still bent, trunk forming a gradual diagonal line, shoulders grounded
phase_descriptions.fim: top controlled pelvic lift, hips higher than in the middle frame, ribs controlled, knees aligned, shoulders and head relaxed on the mat

negative_constraints: do not overarch the lower back
do not flare the ribs aggressively
do not hide foot placement
do not turn it into a fitness glam bridge pose

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: The hips hinge back, knees stay softly bent, the spine stays long, and the load looks centered from midfoot toward heel rather than on the toes.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the hips travel backward first and the torso tips forward while the spine stays long
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the hinge deepens just enough to show the pattern clearly without rounding the back

clarity_checks: the movement reads as a hip hinge, not a squat
the back line remains long and readable
the hips are visibly leading the movement

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing upright, feet hip-width apart, knees softly unlocked, hands crossed over the chest or on the hips, neutral spine, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: hips hinging back, torso angled forward, knees still soft, spine long, chest open
phase_descriptions.fim: clear hip hinge depth without rounding, hips pushed back, torso forward more than in the middle frame, neutral spine, neck aligned

negative_constraints: do not round the back
do not turn it into a barbell exercise
do not lock the knees
do not crop hips or feet

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Support alternates between feet, the trunk stays tall, the step is low impact, and arm swing looks natural rather than athletic or exaggerated.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: one foot lifts slightly and the opposite arm swings while the body stays upright
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the opposite side becomes readable or the lifted step becomes a little clearer without changing the movement into a run

clarity_checks: the image reads instantly as marching in place
foot placement and soft support exchange remain visible
the body posture looks calm and controlled

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing upright, both feet on the floor, arms relaxed naturally, chest open, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: one foot slightly lifted and opposite arm swinging naturally, upright posture, movement controlled and light
phase_descriptions.fim: higher marching moment or opposite side step of the sequence, no jump, neutral spine, calm face

negative_constraints: do not turn it into a run
do not crop feet
do not show jumping
do not exaggerate arm swing

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Support alternates gently between feet, knee lift stays low, the trunk remains relaxed, and the movement reads as recovery intensity rather than cardio work.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: one knee lifts softly with minimal arm swing while the body remains relaxed
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the movement quality remains intentionally similar, with only a subtle shift of support and rhythm

clarity_checks: the movement reads as very light marching
the body language stays relaxed instead of athletic
the frame preserves low-impact intent even if reused

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing in a relaxed marching stance, both feet near the floor, shoulders soft, chest open, calm breathing
phase_descriptions.meio: small gentle knee lift with minimal arm swing, upright posture, low effort movement
phase_descriptions.fim: same easy march quality as the middle frame, low impact, controlled pace, relaxed facial expression

negative_constraints: do not turn it into cardio intensity
do not crop feet
do not show impact or jump
do not use exaggerated athletic posture

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Feet stay grounded, the pelvis remains relatively quiet, the trunk rotates or mobilizes gently, and the shoulders and gaze follow without forcing end range.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the upper trunk begins the mobility action while the base stays calm and the posture remains tall
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the mobility range becomes clearer without turning into a deep twist, backbend, or contortion

clarity_checks: the spine-focused motion is visible without needing text
the pelvis looks stable relative to the trunk
the end range still reads as beginner-friendly

phase_descriptions.inicio: neutral standing position, feet comfortable apart, knees soft, arms relaxed, spine tall, head aligned
phase_descriptions.meio: gentle spinal mobility movement beginning, shoulders and upper back moving while pelvis stays calm, breath controlled
phase_descriptions.fim: clear but gentle spinal mobility end range, movement still beginner-friendly, chest and back readable, no strain in the face

negative_constraints: do not exaggerate twist or backbend
do not hide the spine line with arm placement
do not turn it into a yoga contortion pose
do not crop torso or head

exercises.support_object_visibility: support is optional; if present it must stay visible
exercises.biomechanics_summary: One leg stabilizes the body, one hip explores a small controlled range, the pelvis stays readable, and the trunk remains steady rather than swinging with the leg.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: one hip begins a controlled path while the support leg and trunk stay stable
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the hip reaches a clearer end position without becoming a dance-like or overly large movement

clarity_checks: the moving hip side versus support side are obvious
the pelvis remains readable from the camera angle
any optional support looks helpful but not central

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing neutral base, feet under hips, knees soft, pelvis neutral, chest open, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: one hip starting a controlled mobility path, support leg stable, pelvis readable, upper body steady
phase_descriptions.fim: clear hip mobility end position without strain, trunk controlled, support foot grounded, calm breathing

negative_constraints: do not show a large dance-like range of motion
do not hide the hip line
do not crop feet or knees
do not tilt the torso dramatically

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: A side step opens the base, the arms coordinate upward with low impact, knees stay soft, and there is never an airborne phase.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: one side step opens the stance while the arms move outward and upward in sync
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the body reaches its clearest open position without turning into a jump or high-intensity cardio image

clarity_checks: the image reads as a modified jumping jack without impact
arm and leg coordination are obvious
the open position is clearly distinct from the start position

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing tall, feet together or near together, arms by the sides, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: one side step opening while arms move outward and upward, knees soft, torso upright, no jump
phase_descriptions.fim: widest comfortable open position, feet apart, arms raised or wide, low-impact stance, chest open, gaze forward

negative_constraints: do not show airborne jumping
do not crop fingertips or feet
do not turn it into high-intensity cardio
do not hyperextend the lower back

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object; full mat should remain visible
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Feet and shoulders create the base, hips drive upward, knees stay aligned over the feet, and the ribs stay controlled instead of flaring into lumbar extension.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the hips begin lifting while the shoulders stay anchored and the diagonal body line starts to appear
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the bridge reaches its clearest top position without losing knee alignment or rib control

clarity_checks: the diagonal line from shoulders through hips is readable
foot placement and knee tracking remain visible
the movement reads as glute-driven rather than neck- or lumbar-driven

phase_descriptions.inicio: lying on the back, knees bent, feet flat hip-width apart, arms by the sides, pelvis neutral, head relaxed
phase_descriptions.meio: hips lifting gradually, knees still bent, trunk creating a diagonal line, shoulders grounded, neck relaxed
phase_descriptions.fim: top bridge position, hips clearly higher, knees aligned with feet, ribs controlled, shoulders and head grounded

negative_constraints: do not overarch the lower back
do not flare the rib cage
do not hide foot placement
do not turn it into a glamorous hyperextended bridge

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object; full mat should remain visible
exercises.biomechanics_summary: The same bridge mechanics apply, but the top position is held isometrically with quiet ribs, aligned knees, and a stable diagonal from shoulders to knees.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the bridge rises to its hold position while the base and body line stabilize
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the visual difference is minimal on purpose because the isometric top hold must remain stable and controlled

clarity_checks: the held top position is obviously intentional
the middle and final frame stay stable rather than drifting
the bridge line remains controlled without extra arching

phase_descriptions.inicio: lying on the back, knees bent, feet flat, arms long by the sides, pelvis neutral, head relaxed
phase_descriptions.meio: top bridge position reached and held, hips high, ribs controlled, knees aligned, shoulders anchored
phase_descriptions.fim: same top bridge quality as the middle frame, held with calm control, no extra arching, steady pause

negative_constraints: do not change the frame into a different pose between middle and final
do not overarch the lower back
do not hide the knees or feet
do not turn it into a yoga wheel preparation

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object; full mat line should stay readable
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Hands or forearms and knees form the base, the body creates a line from head to knees, the core braces, and the shoulders stack without lumbar sag or hip pike.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the setup becomes an active support position with the trunk long and the hips aligning between shoulders and knees
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the hold becomes fully stable without visible sagging, piking, or shoulder collapse

clarity_checks: the exercise reads as a modified plank, not a full plank
the line from shoulders to knees remains clear
the support points on the floor stay visible and believable

phase_descriptions.inicio: adapted plank setup on the mat, knees down, hands or forearms under shoulders, trunk long, gaze down
phase_descriptions.meio: body line more engaged, hips aligned between shoulders and knees, abdomen active, neck long
phase_descriptions.fim: stable adapted plank hold, no sagging hips, no piking, shoulders stacked, knees grounded, gaze down slightly forward

negative_constraints: do not arch the lower back
do not lift the hips into a pike
do not crop elbows, knees, or feet
do not turn it into a full advanced plank unless specified

exercises.support_object_visibility: the support surface must stay fully visible and uncropped
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Both palms press into a fixed elevated surface, the body stays in one line from shoulders to heels, elbows bend to bring the chest toward the support, and the support height defines exercise difficulty.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the elbows begin bending and the chest travels toward the support while the body line stays intact
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the bottom position becomes clear without collapsing the hips or changing the exercise into a floor push-up

clarity_checks: the support height and hand placement are obvious
the body reads as one diagonal line rather than segmented
the chest movement toward the support is easy to understand

phase_descriptions.inicio: inclined push-up start with arms extended, hands on support surface, body in a straight diagonal line, heels grounded or feet planted, gaze down slightly forward
phase_descriptions.meio: controlled lowering phase, elbows bending back, chest moving toward the support, body line still straight
phase_descriptions.fim: lowest controlled point of the inclined push-up, chest near the support, elbows bent, core engaged, neck neutral

negative_constraints: do not hide the support surface
do not let the hips sag
do not crop hands or feet
do not turn it into a floor push-up

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: A relaxed base supports calm breathing, the rib cage and trunk show only subtle motion, shoulders stay down, and the whole image should read as reset rather than exercise intensity.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the breath leads a small visible change through the ribs, hands, or upper trunk with no large displacement
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the movement quality remains intentionally subtle so the image still reads as calm breathing and gentle mobility

clarity_checks: the image reads as breathing-focused, not strength or cardio
hands near ribs or chest remain visible
the phase difference stays subtle but intentional

phase_descriptions.inicio: comfortable upright position, shoulders relaxed, chest open, hands placed to support breathing awareness, calm gaze
phase_descriptions.meio: gentle mobility or breathing cue in progress, no large displacement, body relaxed, movement subtle and readable
phase_descriptions.fim: same calm breathing and mobility quality as the middle frame, no athletic intensity, posture soft and accessible

negative_constraints: do not turn it into a workout pose
do not exaggerate the motion
do not crop hands near the rib cage
do not use intense fitness expression

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: The base stays relaxed, the belly and lower ribs expand more than the upper chest, shoulders remain quiet, and the exhale returns the trunk softly to neutral.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the inhalation becomes readable through gentle rib and belly expansion with calm posture
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the exhale softens the rib cage and returns the body toward neutral without dramatic movement

clarity_checks: the image reads as deep breathing rather than posing
hands near the rib cage or belly remain visible
the inhale and exhale states look intentionally different

phase_descriptions.inicio: comfortable upright stance, shoulders relaxed, hands lightly placed to guide breathing, chest open, calm gaze
phase_descriptions.meio: inhalation emphasis, rib cage gently expanding, posture relaxed, face calm
phase_descriptions.fim: soft exhalation emphasis, shoulders still relaxed, neutral spine, no dramatic movement

negative_constraints: do not turn it into a yoga performance pose
do not exaggerate arm movement
do not crop the hands
do not create a strained facial expression

exercises.support_object_visibility: no support object
exercises.biomechanics_summary: One foot steps laterally, weight shifts sideways, the other foot taps in, the trunk stays upright, and the movement remains light with no jump phase.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: one foot steps out and the body weight begins shifting laterally while the opposite foot prepares to follow
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the tap-in becomes clear and completes the side step without turning the image into a dance pose or jump

clarity_checks: the side step direction is obvious
the weight shift and tap-in role are readable
the image reads as low-impact lateral movement

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing upright, feet near together, knees soft, arms relaxed, chest open, gaze forward
phase_descriptions.meio: one foot stepping out to the side, weight shifting laterally, opposite foot preparing to tap in, arms moving naturally
phase_descriptions.fim: side step completed with the other foot touching in, body still upright, movement light and rhythmic, no jump

negative_constraints: do not show airborne jumping
do not crop feet
do not exaggerate hip sway
do not turn it into a dance performance pose

exercises.support_object_visibility: wall edge and both hands contacting the wall must remain visible
exercises.biomechanics_summary: Both palms press into a vertical wall, the body stays in one straight line from head to heels, elbows bend to bring the chest forward, and the wall plane remains consistent across frames.

phase_delta.inicio_to_meio: the elbows begin bending and the chest travels toward the wall while the body remains aligned
phase_delta.meio_to_fim: the closest controlled wall position becomes clear without sagging the hips or changing the support logic

clarity_checks: wall contact points remain fully visible
the body line and hand spacing are easy to read
the image reads as a wall push-up rather than a desk or floor push-up

phase_descriptions.inicio: standing facing the wall in a slight 3/4 side view, arms extended, hands on the wall at chest height, body in a straight line from head to heels
phase_descriptions.meio: controlled lowering toward the wall, elbows bending, chest moving forward, heels grounded, trunk aligned
phase_descriptions.fim: closest controlled position to the wall, elbows more bent, body still straight, hands fully visible on the wall, gaze slightly down

negative_constraints: do not hide the wall contact point
do not let the hips sag
do not crop hands or feet
do not turn it into a desk push-up or floor push-up
